summary: Provision new EKS clusters, managed node groups, and Fargate profiles. Every stand-up routes to an engineer for review — day-2 operations on existing EKS resources land in a later wave.

operations.title: Provision a new EKS cluster
operations.summary: Request a brand-new EKS cluster; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new Kubernetes control plane as a complete specification: version, IAM role, network placement, endpoint access, and admin bootstrap. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: cluster_name
params.label: Cluster name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like app-tier-eks. Names the cluster in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: k8s_version
params.label: Kubernetes version
params.help: Leave the estate's current default selected unless a workload needs a specific version.

params.name: cluster_role
params.label: Cluster IAM role
params.help: The IAM role EKS assumes to manage the cluster's AWS resources (the EKS cluster service role).

params.name: subnet_ids
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Where the control plane's elastic network interfaces live. Span at least two Availability Zones.

params.name: security_group_ids
params.label: Additional security groups
params.help: Extra groups applied to the control plane's network interfaces, beyond the cluster's own managed one.

params.name: endpoint_private_access
params.label: Private API endpoint
params.help: Lets resources inside the VPC reach the Kubernetes API without crossing the public internet.

params.name: endpoint_public_access
params.label: Public API endpoint
params.help: Off keeps the API server reachable only from inside the VPC — the estate default.

params.name: authentication_mode
params.label: Authentication mode
params.help: API uses EKS access entries for authorization; the others also honor the legacy aws-auth ConfigMap.

params.name: bootstrap_admin_permissions
params.label: Grant creator admin access
params.help: Grants the creating principal cluster-admin at bootstrap — the common starting point for a new cluster.

params.name: enabled_cluster_log_types
params.label: Control plane logs
params.help: Which control plane log streams EKS exports to CloudWatch Logs. Leave blank to export none.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this cluster does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this cluster.

keywords: stand up a k8s control plane
decisions: Add-ons (VPC CNI, CoreDNS, kube-proxy, EBS/EFS CSI drivers) are installed as an engineer follow-up after creation
Encryption of Kubernetes secrets with a customer-managed KMS key (encryption_config) is an engineer decision
Fine-grained access entries beyond the bootstrap admin grant are configured after the cluster exists

operations.title: Provision a new EKS managed node group
operations.summary: Request a brand-new managed node group; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new group of worker nodes as a complete specification: which cluster it joins, instance sizing, capacity type, and scaling bounds. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: node_group_name
params.label: Node group name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like app-tier-ng. Names the node group in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: cluster
params.label: Cluster
params.help: The EKS cluster this node group joins. Create the cluster first (eks-cluster-create) if none exists yet.

params.name: node_role
params.label: Node IAM role
params.help: The IAM role each worker node's EC2 instance profile assumes (the EKS node role).

params.name: subnet_ids
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Where worker node instances launch. Pick subnets spanning at least two Availability Zones.

params.name: capacity_type
params.label: Capacity type
params.help: SPOT is cheaper but nodes can be reclaimed on short notice.

params.name: ami_type
params.label: AMI type
params.help: The managed worker-node operating system image family.

params.name: instance_types
params.label: Instance type
params.help: The EC2 instance type worker nodes launch as.

params.name: disk_size
params.label: Node disk size (GiB)
params.help: Root volume size for each worker node.

params.name: desired_size
params.label: Desired size
params.help: Starting number of worker nodes.

params.name: min_size
params.label: Minimum size
params.help: Fewest worker nodes the group ever runs.

params.name: max_size
params.label: Maximum size
params.help: Most worker nodes the group may scale up to.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this node group does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this node group.

decisions: A custom launch template (for extra EBS volumes, user data, or a golden AMI) is an engineer decision
Kubernetes labels and taints (beyond the AWS-managed defaults) are configured as an engineer follow-up
Remote SSH access (remote_access) is off by default — an engineer decision if node-level shell access is required

operations.title: Provision a new EKS Fargate profile
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Fargate profile; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new Fargate profile as a complete specification: which cluster it attaches to, its private subnets, and the namespace it schedules pods for. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: fargate_profile_name
params.label: Fargate profile name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, like batch-jobs-fp. Names the profile in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: cluster
params.label: Cluster
params.help: The EKS cluster this Fargate profile attaches to. Create the cluster first (eks-cluster-create) if none exists yet.

params.name: pod_execution_role
params.label: Pod execution role
params.help: The IAM role Fargate uses to make AWS API calls on behalf of pods scheduled by this profile.

params.name: subnet_ids
params.label: Subnets
params.help: Private subnets pods launch into. Fargate pods need private egress (NAT), never a public subnet.

params.name: namespace
params.label: Kubernetes namespace
params.help: Pods in this namespace are scheduled on Fargate by this profile.

params.name: labels_note
params.label: Pod label selector
params.help: An optional Kubernetes label key/value selector narrowing which pods in the namespace this profile covers — an engineer adds it to match the workload's pod spec.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this Fargate profile does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this Fargate profile.

decisions: The pod label selector narrowing this profile beyond the whole namespace is an engineer decision
Additional selectors (multiple namespace/label pairs) beyond the one authored here are an engineer follow-up
